steps.title: Test the prediction
steps.content: The researcher's claim predicts that languages farther from Africa should retain fewer sounds from the original language. A weakening finding should show the opposite pattern: languages outside Africa having more sounds than languages in Africa.

steps.title: Reject supporting patterns
steps.content: Choice A supports greater sound variety in Africa. Choice C fits the distance pattern by putting fewer sounds farther away. Choice D does not directly compare Africa with farther regions. A finding that Europe and Western Asia tend to have more sounds than Africa reverses the prediction. The answer is B.
